methods.description: Conducting fieldwork at the Karbindo coal mine to measure stratigraphic sections and collect rock samples for subsequent analysis (palynological and XRD).

steps.description: The fieldwork was conducted at the Karbindo coal mine to measure stratigraphic section and to collect rock samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct fieldwork, measure stratigraphic section, and collect rock samples at a specific location (Karbindo coal mine).
inputs: Field equipment (for measuring section, collecting samples)

methods.description: A micropaleontological technique applied to rock samples to extract, process, and observe palynomorphs (pollen and spores) for biostratigraphic dating, correlation, and interpretation of the depositional environment, particularly indicating proximity to coastlines based on species presence. This method is used for dating and correlation of non-marine sediments.

steps.description: involved extraction from 100-gram samples was carried out using HF and HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract palynomorphs from 100-gram rock samples using hydrofluoric acid (HF) and hydrochloric acid (HCl).

steps.description: Palynomorphs were separated from the substrate using a zinc chloride solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate palynomorphs from substrate using zinc chloride solution (density separation).

steps.description: The residue was then sieved with a five Ɋm mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve the palynomorph residue using a 5 micrometer mesh.

steps.description: and mounted on slides with glycerin jelly.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount the sieved palynomorph residue onto microscope slides using glycerin jelly.

steps.description: Palynomorphs observation was done using 1000X magnification light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and identify palynomorphs on mounted slides using a light microscope at 1000X magnification.

methods.description: X-ray Diffraction analysis performed on rock samples to characterize their mineralogical composition, specifically focusing on carbonate and pyrite content as indicators of marine influence on the depositional environment.

steps.description: Furthermore, XRD analysis was performed for mineralogical composition characterization for each sample, particularly for the carbonate and pyrite content which can indicate the marine incursion to the depositional environment. XRD analysis also performed in GeolLabs.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform X-ray Diffraction (XRD) analysis on rock samples to determine mineralogical composition, focusing on carbonate and pyrite content.
outputs: Mineralogical composition data (XRD patterns, identified minerals, relative abundance of carbonate/pyrite)
